items.title: Roep om andere status Serie A-arbiters: 'Moeten profs zijn'
items.summary: Juventus voorkwam diep in blessuretijd een nederlaag tegen Lazio , toch was Teun Koopmeiners na afloop niet helemaal tevreden. De middenvelder zag een doelpunt worden afgekeurd. Ook Luciano Spalletti was uitgesproken, maar vooral over de arbitrage.

items.title: Crisis Anderlecht verdiept: nog maar twee stafleden over
items.summary: Anderlecht blijft in zwaar weer verkeren. Na het ontslag van hoofdtrainer Besnik Hasi is ook interim-coach Edward Still alweer vertrokken. Still nam na Hasi's exit tijdelijk het roer over, maar kon het tij niet keren: onder zijn leiding verloor Paars-Wit twee wedstrijden op rij.

items.title: De opvallende cijfers van gele kaarten-koning Wellenreuther
items.summary: Feyenoord-doelman Timon Wellenreuther ontving zondag tijdens het uitduel met FC Utrecht voor de 22ste keer een gele kaart in een Eredivisie-wedstrijd. Daarmee scherpte de Duitser zijn eigen Eredivisie-record verder aan. Ook bij Feyenoord bereikte hij een opmerkelijke mijlpaal met zijn volgende gele kaart.

items.title: Elftal van de Week: hofleverancier Twente profiteert van offday topclubs
items.summary: FC Twente is na de 22 ste speelronde met overmacht hofleverancier van het Elftal van de Week. Na de doelpuntrijke overwinning op SC Heerenveen staan liefst zes spelers van de Tukkers in het elftal met Eredivisie-uitblinkers.

items.title: Rondje Eredivisie: PSV de snelste kampioen?
items.summary: Waar PSV blijft winnen, leed Ajax weer puntenverlies. Feyenoord daarentegen maakte in Utrecht een einde aan een zwakke serie . We bespreken deze zaken en nog veel meer in een nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Eredivisie.

items.title: 'Just let it go!' | Should Man City's third goal have stood vs Liverpool?
items.summary: Ref Watch takes a closer look at the chaotic, last-gasp VAR drama at Anfield and asks whether or not referee Craig Pawson should have allowed Manchester City's third goal to stand and NOT send off Dominik Szoboszlai.

items.title: ‘We’re like a family here’: Habib Diarra delights in good times at Sunderland
items.summary: Club’s record signing on adjusting to the north-east, Afcon pride, and learning from Patrick Vieira and Liam Rosenior Vous or Tu? It says a lot about Habib Diarra that his joy at being promoted from Strasbourg’s Under-17s to the first team was tempered by anxiety over the two French words for “you.” Would addressing new, senior teammates by using “tu” be regarded as disrespectful? Ultimately, the young midfielder played safe and opted for the more formal “vous”. Cue wholesale laughter from the older players who told him not to be so silly; he was one of them now. Continue reading...

items.title: Ref Watch LIVE! Szoboszlai red, Guehi DOGSO and Newcastle handball analysed
Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Liverpool rue costly mistakes, Viktor Gyökeres builds up a head of steam and Rayan gets the hype train chugging Premier League top scorers: the latest standings Arne Slot was close to landing a coup against Pep Guardiola, the coach he admires most. Then came more of the individual errors that have ruined Liverpool’s title defence. Aching weaknesses within Slot’s squad were exposed again. Dominik Szoboszlai playing Bernardo Silva onside for Manchester City’s equaliser was an error midfielders playing full-back will make. Szoboszlai’s late red card was, though, foolish. Alisson’s foul on Matheus Nunes for Erling Haaland’s decisive penalty was another rush of blood. Liverpool’s huge summer spend was motivated by their executives’ belief in buying the best individuals to unlock the Premier League’s tactical cages. City’s key individuals showed such a policy can pay off, with Silva inspirational, Gianluigi Donnarumma making the save that sparked the game’s chaotic final scenes, Marc Guéhi looking an astute defensive signing and Haaland supplying Silva’s goal. City had been unconvincing but their mentality held, allowing them to eventually profit from Hugo Ekitiké’s misses and the waning of Mohamed Salah. John Brewin Match report: Liverpool 1-2 Manchester City Match report: Brighton 0-1 Crystal Palace Match report: Arsenal 3-0 Sunderland Match report: Newcastle 2-3 Brentford Continue reading...

items.summary: Coaching changes, new squads, stars on show and up-and-comers with a point to prove. The Super League 2026 season is about to begin and, with it, storylines take shape.

items.summary: After Liverpool's loss, coach Arne Slot fumed that Man City deserved a red card: Is he right?

items.title: Man City's comeback thriller keeps Premier League title hopes alive; Liverpool slip further
items.summary: Liverpool looked set to win, but Man City's two unanswered goals mean the Premier League title race stays alive and Liverpool's Champions League hopes are in doubt.

items.title: Haaland earns City dramatic late win at Liverpool
items.summary: Erling Haaland kept Manchester City in the title race with an added-time penalty as Pep Guardiola's side came from behind to win 2-1 in a chaotic finish.

items.title: Pep warns Arsenal: 'A lot can happen' in title race
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has warned Arsenal that 'a lot can happen in 13 games' as the Premier League title race heats up.

items.title: Arbeloa: Mbappé can match Ronaldo at Madrid
items.summary: Coach Álvaro Arbeloa said Kylian Mbappé "has the qualities" to match the "out of this world" Cristiano Ronaldo's impact at Real Madrid.

items.title: European football: PSG thrash Marseille and return to summit of Ligue 1
items.summary: Dembélé doubles up in 5-0 mauling Kane and Díaz on target in Bayern win Ousmane Dembélé struck twice as Paris Saint-Germain blew away bitter rivals Marseille on Sunday, reclaiming top spot in Ligue 1 with a crushing 5-0 victory at the Parc des Princes. Dembélé opened the scoring after just 12 minutes and added a second before half-time as PSG delivered a real statement of intent going into the crucial months of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: Rangers to host Celtic in Scottish Cup quarter-finals
items.summary: Rangers host Old Firm rivals Celtic and holders Aberdeen face a potential trip to Dunfermline in the Scottish Cup quarter-finals.
